--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,48 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: fc80
+project: builtin-commands
+title: Surface per-view "Switch to View «name»" in the View context menu (and verify palette coverage everywhere)
+---
+## Problem
+
+Right-clicking a View (the left-nav view buttons) shows no usable context menu — at minimum it should offer **"Switch to View «name»"**. Separately, every view should be switchable from the command palette anywhere ("Switch to View «name»"), as dynamic commands derived from the available views.
+
+This is a deliberate-decision reversal, not a pure bug. Today view switching is **palette-only by design**:
+
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs` → `emit_view_switch` emits one dynamic `view.set` row per view (`name: "Switch to {view.name}"`, `args: { view_id }`) but with **`context_menu: false`**, so it never appears on right-click.
+- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/left-nav.tsx` documents this verbatim: *"View switching is palette-only, so the context menu never shows a 'Switch to <ViewName>' entry; the `view:{id}` moniker is still needed for other dynamics (e.g. `entity.add:{type}`)."* Each view button already mounts a `CommandScopeProvider moniker={view:{id}}` and reads `useContextMenu`, so the scope plumbing exists — only the backend emission withholds the entry. (See also the regression marker `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/view-switch-commands.retired.node.test.ts`.)
+
+The dynamic rows are NOT collapsed by dedup — `SeenKey = (id, target, args)` includes the args serialization (`scope_commands.rs:233`), so per-view `view.set` rows (same id, distinct `view_id` args) are distinct. The per-perspective `perspective.switch` dynamic uses this exact same-id/different-args pattern and works.
+
+## Critical constraint
+
+`emit_view_switch` emits **unconditionally** (all views, independent of scope). Naively flipping `context_menu: true` on it would make every view-switch row appear in EVERY context menu app-wide (right-click a task, column, anything). The context-menu emission must be **scope-gated to a view context** — mirror `emit_entity_add`, which only emits when a `view:{id}` moniker is present in the scope chain.
+
+## What
+
+One cohesive change: make per-view "Switch to View «name»" available in (a) the View context menu and (b) the palette everywhere.
+
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/scope_commands.rs`:
+  - Keep the existing palette emission (all views, `context_menu: false`) so every view is palette-switchable.
+  - Add a **scope-gated** context-menu emission: when a `view:{id}` moniker is in `scope_chain`, emit `view.set` rows with `context_menu: true` for the available views so right-clicking a view (or within a view) offers "Switch to View «name»". Decide and document the set: recommended default is **all views** (so you can jump to any from the menu) gated on the `view:` context; if task/column context menus become noisy, tighten the gate to the left-nav marker (`ui:left-nav`)/the leaf `view:` moniker. Avoid double-naming when `view.name` already contains "View" (caption should read naturally, e.g. `Switch to View {{view.name}}` vs `Switch to {{view.name}}`).
+  - Wire the new emission into `emit_dynamic_commands` (alongside `emit_view_switch`).
+- `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/left-nav.tsx`: update the now-stale "view switching is palette-only … context menu never shows a Switch to <ViewName> entry" docstring; verify `ViewButton`'s `useContextMenu` renders the new context_menu rows for its `view:{id}` scope.
+- Verify the palette path populates `DynamicSources.views` so the switch rows are available "anywhere" (not only when a view is focused). `DynamicSources.views` is built by `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/dynamic_sources.rs::build_dynamic_sources`; confirm the palette's `commands_for_scope` call receives a populated `dynamic` with `.views` regardless of current focus, and fix if it passes `None`/empty.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Right-clicking a view in the left-nav shows a context menu containing "Switch to View «name»" (at least the entry to switch to that/those view(s)); selecting it dispatches `view.set` with the correct `view_id` and switches the active view.
+- [ ] The command palette lists a "Switch to View «name»" entry for every available view, dispatchable from anywhere in the app (not only while a view is focused).
+- [ ] View-switch rows do NOT appear in unrelated context menus (right-click a task/column/board surface with no `view:` relevance shows no view-switch entries) — i.e. the context-menu emission is scope-gated.
+- [ ] No new backend command id; reuses the existing `view.set` (kanban-misc-commands) with per-view `args.view_id` and `{{view.name}}`-templated captions.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/dynamic_sources_headless.rs` (the headless harness that asserts the exact dynamic rows `commands_for_scope` emits): assert that with a `view:{id}` moniker in scope, `view.set` rows are emitted with `context_menu: true` (one per available view, distinct `view_id` args, correct captions), AND that with NO `view:` moniker in scope no `context_menu: true` view-switch rows are emitted. Keep asserting the palette (`context_menu: false`) rows for all views.
+- [ ] `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/left-nav.view-switch.browser.test.tsx`: extend to open the context menu on a view button and assert a "Switch to View «name»" item is present and dispatches `view.set` with the right `view_id`.
+- [ ] Update/replace `apps/kanban-app/ui/src/components/view-switch-commands.retired.node.test.ts` to reflect the un-retired context-menu behavior (or delete it if fully superseded), so the retired-surface marker doesn't contradict the new behavior.
+- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-kanban --test dynamic_sources_headless` and `cd apps/kanban-app/ui && npx vitest run src/components/left-nav.view-switch.browser.test.tsx` both pass (new assertions red before the change, green after).
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — add the failing headless assertion (context_menu:true view-switch when `view:` in scope) and the failing left-nav context-menu test first, then implement the scope-gated emission and make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file
